metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of new medicinal products for the treatment of acute coronary syndrome

rules.rule_text: Definitions of clinical endpoints in confirmatory trials must be in line with the relevant clinical guidelines (ESC) to facilitate interpretation and allow comparisons across studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Definitions of clinical endpoints in confirmatory trials should be in line with the relevant clinical guidelines [3, 4, 10, 11 and 12] to facilitate interpretation of the results, to allow comparisons across clinical studies and to extrapolate to clinical practice.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of mortality in confirmatory trials must include both all-cause mortality and cardiovascular mortality.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of mortality in confirmatory trials should include both all-cause mortality and cardiovascular mortality (see section 5.1).

rules.rule_text: New onset MI is a relevant endpoint in ACS studies and should always be investigated. The criteria of MI are the same as those used to define the index event, per the universal definition of MI.
rules.verbatim_quote: New onset MI is a relevant endpoint in studies of ACS and should always be investigated. The definition of MI has evolved through the years; at the time of drafting of this Guideline, the third universal definition of MI is applicable [10]. Criteria of MI are the same as those used to define the index event (see below).

rules.rule_text: Non-fatal stroke should only be investigated as part of the composite endpoint if there is a biological rationale suggesting a beneficial or detrimental treatment effect on stroke risk. Otherwise, including it may add complexity and noise.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-fatal stroke should be investigated only if there is a biological rationale suggesting a beneficial or detrimental treatment effect on risk of stroke [13]. Otherwise, including non-fatal stroke as part of the composite endpoint may add complexity and noise rather than clarity.

rules.rule_text: Revascularisation endpoints included as part of a primary composite must be clearly justified, pre-defined, systematically assessed, and independently adjudicated.
rules.verbatim_quote: their inclusion as a primary endpoint should be clearly justified and their assessment pre-defined and systematically assessed. Independent adjudication of these endpoints is necessary.

rules.rule_text: Unstable angina necessitating hospitalisation is not encouraged to be included in the composite primary endpoint due to varying definitions and subjectivity.
rules.verbatim_quote: due to the varying definitions used, the associated subjectivity and the influence of local clinical practice, this endpoint is not encouraged to be included in the composite primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Stent thrombosis investigation as part of the primary endpoint is not encouraged, except for the 'definite' subcategory. Stent thrombosis should be collected as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: The investigation of stent thrombosis as part of the primary endpoint is not encouraged due to the uncertainty of the clinical relevance of all captured events, except for the "definite" subcategory which could be of more clinical relevance than the other subtypes.
rules.exceptions: Definite stent thrombosis subcategory may be acceptable.

rules.rule_text: The preferred composite endpoint is CV death and non-fatal MI. Non-fatal stroke may be included if justified. Subjective outcomes in the same composite are generally not encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is preferred to investigate the composite of (cardiovascular) death and non-fatal MI in confirmatory studies; non-fatal stroke could be included in the composite if justified. ... The inclusion of subjective outcomes in the same composite is generally not encouraged, as they may either drive the efficacy or dilute the results.
rules.exceptions: If subjective endpoints are included, they must be stringently defined and adjudicated.

rules.rule_text: Each component of the primary composite endpoint must be analysed as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each component of the primary composite endpoint should be analysed as secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Complete recanalization cannot be considered as a surrogate for survival when assessing fibrinolytic drugs. All-cause mortality or a combined endpoint is the most relevant endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: complete recanalization cannot be considered as a surrogate for survival when assessing fibrinolytic drugs, as some medicinal products providing higher complete recanalization rates than alteplase, failed to demonstrate additional survival benefit. For this reason, all-cause mortality is the most relevant endpoint or a combined endpoint as previously discussed.

rules.rule_text: All-cause mortality may in many cases be the preferred choice over CV mortality for the primary endpoint. When CV death is used, all-cause mortality must be planned and presented as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: all-cause mortality may in many cases be the preferred choice. ... In this case, all-cause mortality should be planned and presented as secondary endpoint (or as a component of a composite endpoint).
rules.exceptions: CV death acceptable as primary when non-CV death incidence is expected to be negligible.

rules.rule_text: It is mandatory to report and centrally adjudicate all mortality data in all studies in patients with ACS. Specific cause of death must be defined.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is mandatory to report and centrally adjudicate all mortality data in all studies in patients with ACS. Efforts should be made to define the specific cause of death occurring in the studies (e.g. sudden cardiac death, pump failure, acute coronary events).

rules.rule_text: MI diagnosis requires detection of rise and/or fall of cardiac biomarker values (preferably cardiac troponin) with at least one value above the 99th percentile upper reference limit. All MIs must be collected and classified by subtype.
rules.verbatim_quote: The diagnostic of MI is based on the detection of a rise and/or fall of cardiac biomarker values [preferably cardiac troponin (cTn)] with at least one value above the 99th percentile upper reference limit (URL). All MIs should be collected and also classified by their different sub types.

rules.rule_text: Post-procedural MIs should be presented with higher cut-off values (>=5 and >=10x upper level of normal ULN, or CK-MB >=70x ULN of cTn) to support clinical relevance.
rules.verbatim_quote: to support the clinical relevance of post procedural MIs, these events should be presented with higher cut-off values (≥ 5 and ≥10x upper level of normal ULN, in case of CK-MB or ≥70x ULN of cTn) [19].

rules.rule_text: All-cause stroke (not only ischaemic) should be used as part of the composite endpoint. Rates of ischaemic and haemorrhagic strokes must be presented separately.
rules.verbatim_quote: the use of all-cause stroke as part of the composite endpoint is recommended. ... To differentiate between effects on efficacy and safety, rates of ischemic and haemorrhagic strokes should be presented separately.

rules.rule_text: Stroke diagnosis must be confirmed by brain imaging. A suspected TIA should be adjudicated as ischaemic stroke if positive neuroimaging confirms cerebral infarction, even if symptoms last less than 24 hours.
rules.verbatim_quote: Diagnosis of stroke... should be confirmed by brain imaging. ... A suspected transient ischemic attack (TIA) should be adjudicated as ischaemic stroke if there is positive neuroimaging confirming a cerebral infarction, even if the duration of symptoms is of less than 24 hours duration.

rules.rule_text: Stent thrombosis must be collected and classified as definite, probable, and possible per accepted definitions. Timing (acute, sub-acute, late, very late) must be documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stent thrombosis (ST) should be collected and classified as definite, probable and possible in line with acceptable definitions [21]. In addition, the timing of ST should be documented (acute, sub-acute, late and very late), as risk factors and clinical sequels differ with timing.

rules.rule_text: Angiograms must undergo central blinded reading. TIMI 3 flow at 90 minutes is the most relevant angiographic endpoint for fibrinolytic drugs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Angiograms should undergo central blinded reading. In principle, the rate of TIMI 3 flow (complete revascularization) of the infarct related artery at 90 minutes is considered the most relevant angiographic endpoint, as it has been shown to correlate with an improved outcome in terms of mortality and left ventricular function.

rules.rule_text: Inclusion of both STE-ACS/STEMI and NSTE-ACS/NSTEMI patients in the same trial must be justified based on mechanism of action and proposed time of intervention. Both subgroups must be well represented.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Inclusion criteria for the therapeutic studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Inclusion of both STE-ACS/ STEMI and NSTE-ACS/NSTEMI patients in the same clinical trial (or not) should be justified based on the mechanism of action of the investigated product and the proposed time of intervention. If both subgroups are investigated in the same trial, both subgroups should be well represented.

rules.rule_text: Time of inclusion of patients in relation to the index event must be set and adequately discussed a priori.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Inclusion criteria for the therapeutic studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Time of inclusion of the patients in relation to the index event should be set and adequately discussed a priori.

rules.rule_text: STEMI and NSTEMI patients should preferably be investigated in separate clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the investigation of interventions in these patients should preferably be performed in separate clinical trials.
rules.exceptions: Post-acute/longer term interventions aimed at secondary prevention or plaque stabilisation may justify a combined trial.

rules.rule_text: Exclusion criteria should not be defined too narrowly. Polymorbid patients (renal/hepatic impairment, heart failure) should not automatically be excluded from main therapeutic trials.
rules.source_section: 6.3 Exclusion criteria for the therapeutic studies
rules.verbatim_quote: For reasons of generalisability of the study results to the future target population it is strongly advised not to define the exclusion criteria too narrow, i.e. polymorbid patients (e.g. renal and/or hepatic impairment, heart failure), should not automatically be excluded from the main therapeutic clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Risk scores (GRACE, TIMI) should either be reported or adequate data provided in study files to enable risk score calculations. Risk-based analyses must be pre-defined and foreseen in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: From a regulatory perspective, risk scores should either be reported or adequate data should be provided in the study files to enable risk score calculations. ... Analysis among different risk setting using risk scores should be pre-defined and foreseen in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Adequate representation of older patients (above 70 years) in clinical trials must be ensured.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate representation of older patients (above 70 years) in the clinical trials should be ensured.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory studies must be controlled, randomised, with every effort to maintain double blindness. The statistical approach (superiority, equivalence, non-inferiority) must be pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies aiming at providing proof of efficacy must have a confirmatory statistical approach. These studies must be controlled, randomised and every effort should be made to maintain double blindness. The statistical approach e.g. a demonstration of superiority, equivalence or non-inferiority, has to be pre-specified in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Investigation of all-cause mortality and long-term follow-up are mandatory in studies with novel interventions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Investigation of all-cause mortality and long term follow-up are mandatory in studies with novel interventions.

rules.rule_text: Background therapy must reflect the standard of care as recommended by current clinical guidelines. The comparator choice should reflect EU standard of care and be unified across regions.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, background therapy should reflect the standard of care as recommended by current clinical guidelines [3, 4]. ... The choice of the comparator should reflect the EU standard of care and should preferably be unified throughout the different regions.

rules.rule_text: For short-term administration products, the primary endpoint should preferably be measured at 30 days. Follow-up measurements at 180 days and at study drug termination are also expected.
rules.verbatim_quote: For medicinal products intended for short-term administration (e.g. hours to 7 days), the primary endpoint should preferably be measured at 30 days following initiation of therapy in the confirmatory studies. ... further measurements should be performed after longer (e.g. 180 days) but also after shorter duration (e.g. at time of termination of study drugs) as secondary measures of efficacy.
rules.exceptions: Shorter time spans acceptable if follow-up data provide evidence of durability.

rules.rule_text: Randomisation must be stratified for the qualifying condition (STEMI and NSTEMI) and for region. Stratification factors must be reflected in the statistical analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomisation should be stratified for the qualifying condition (STEMI and NSTEMI) and for region (if applicable). Other risk factors (gender, age) may also be considered for stratification of the randomisation. Stratification factors should be reflected in the statistical analysis.

rules.rule_text: In hierarchical composite analysis, all-cause mortality is the most relevant component and an over-mortality cannot be compensated by a decreased rate of other events.
rules.verbatim_quote: In a hierarchical view, the component "all cause mortality" will be considered as being the most relevant (e.g. an over-mortality cannot be compensated by a decreased rate of angina pectoris).

rules.rule_text: Bleeding events must be adjudicated by a central independent and blinded committee using pre-specified limits. In pivotal trials, major bleeding is the recommended primary safety endpoint. Bleeding must be reported using at least two acceptable classification systems (e.g., GUSTO, TIMI, BARC).
rules.verbatim_quote: the adjudication of bleeding events by a central independent and blinded committee of experts, using pre-specified limits and clear terms of reference is strongly encouraged. ... In pivotal trials, the recommended primary safety endpoint is major bleeding... Reporting of bleeding events using two acceptable definitions e.g., GUSTO, TIMI and BARC definitions [23] could be considered for future clinical trials and/or regulatory submissions.

rules.rule_text: Studies must evaluate rebound effect after termination of the study drug, including clear increase in angina pectoris, new MI, death, and other thrombotic events.
rules.verbatim_quote: The studies should include the evaluation of events which are considered to be characteristic for a possible rebound effect (e.g. clear increase in angina pectoris and/or new MI and/or death and/or other thrombotic events) after termination of the study drug.
